name: Cefazolin
description: A semisynthetic cephalosporin analog with broad-spectrum antibiotic action due to inhibition of bacterial cell wall synthesis. It attains high serum levels and is excreted quickly via the urine.
indication: Mainly used to treat bacterial infections of the skin. It can also be used to treat moderately severe bacterial infections involving the lung, bone, joint, stomach, blood, heart valve, and urinary tract. It is clinically effective against infections caused by staphylococci and streptococci species of Gram positive bacteria. May be used for surgical prophylaxis; if required metronidazole may be added to cover B. fragilis.
mechanismOfAction: In vitro tests demonstrate that the bactericidal action of cephalosporins results from inhibition of cell wall synthesis. By binding to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, it inhibits the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins.
pharmacodynamics: Cefazolin (also known as cefazoline or cephazolin) is a semi-synthetic first generation cephalosporin for parenteral administration. Cefazolin has broad-spectrum antibiotic action due to inhibition of bacterial cell wall synthesis. It attains high serum levels and is excreted quickly via the urine.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefazolin.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefazolin.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefazolin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefazolin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.

interactions.name: Fosphenytoin
interactions.description: The protein binding of Fosphenytoin can be decreased when combined with Cefazolin.

interactions.name: Phenytoin
interactions.description: The protein binding of Phenytoin can be decreased when combined with Cefazolin.

interactions.name: Teriflunomide
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefazolin can be increased when it is combined with Teriflunomide.
